evidence_boundary: The core positioning claims are present in the paper and linked to evidence-boundary artifacts, but they do not substitute for real mobile-tier or baseline experiments.

claims.statement: Phone-native AI coding should be framed as a harness control plane, not as compressing a full IDE onto a phone.
paper_terms: not as ``putting a full IDE on a phone''
not served well by compressing a desktop IDE into a small screen
claims.boundary: This is a positioning claim, not an empirical performance result.

claims.statement: The research object is the harness layer that coordinates mobile inputs, artifacts, runtime or delivery routes, and evidence.
paper_terms: The harness is the research object
claims.boundary: The current implementation and benchmark support the abstraction, while real mobile performance remains open.

claims.statement: MobileHarnessBench is scoped to phone-native AI coding harnesses rather than general app-control phone-use agents.
paper_terms: not ask whether an agent can operate arbitrary consumer apps
claims.boundary: The scope is intentionally narrower than general phone-use benchmarks.

claims.statement: Benchmark claims count evidence, traces, verifiers and mobile-tier artifacts rather than task intention or generated text.
paper_terms: A mobile benchmark must count evidence, not intention
A task is counted only when its verifier result, trace, summary, artifacts, and required mobile-tier evidence are present
claims.boundary: Only T0 fixture evidence is counted in the current draft; mobile and baseline results remain open.
